2026-04-24T21:54:06.4023707Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4024228Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4024647Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4025008Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4025303Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4025553Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4025710Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4025898Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4026069Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4026298Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4026497Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4026767Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-24T21:54:06.4027020Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. 2026-04-24T21:57:12.9994343Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-24T21:54:57.458040766Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58668,\"title\":\"[BUG] DiagnosticSetting missing required Scope property\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Provisioning.Monitor\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhile a scope property isn\\u0026#39;t required for DiagnosticSetting resources, omitting it would just bind the diagnostic setting to the resource group during deployment which could be unintended by the user. In our case, we are trying to add it to a sql database which requires something like the below code to force in the scope property.\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar scopeValue = new BicepValue(new IdentifierExpression(database.BicepIdentifier));\\n((IBicepValue)scopeValue).Self = new BicepValueReference(diagnosticSetting, \\u0026#34;Scope\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;]);\\ndiagnosticSetting.ProvisionableProperties[\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;] = scopeValue;\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe DiagnosticSetting class should support a Scope field to allow binding the resource to the correct parent resource.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nDepending on whether you force in the scope field or not, you could end up with a diagnostic setting on the resource group unintentionally.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nCreate a simple Aspire application using a default template.\\nAdd a sql server resource and a database\\nInside the ConfigureInfrastructure callback (on the server), add the diagnostic setting, then just do `aspire publish` to get the generated bicep.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; You\\u0026#39;ll need to have a dev environment setup to run Aspire projects. If all you\\u0026#39;re doing is executing aspire publish the Aspire CLI would be sufficient. If you\\u0026#39;re going to run the project you\\u0026#39;ll need Docker, at least for how the below code is written.\\n\\nSample snippet - add this in your Aspire App Host\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar laWorkspace = builder.AddAzureLogAnalyticsWorkspace(\\u0026#34;log-analytics\\u0026#34;)\\nvar azureSql = builder.AddAzureSqlServer(\\u0026#34;azuresql\\u0026#34;).RunAsContainer(c =\\u0026gt; c.WithLifetime(ContainerLifetime.Persistent));\\nvar db = azureSql.AddDatabase(\\u0026#34;mydb\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;MyDB\\u0026#34;);\\nazureSql.ConfigureInfrastructure(infra =\\u0026gt;\\n  {\\n      var database = infra.GetProvisionableResources().OfType().Single();\\n      var diagnosticSetting = new DiagnosticSetting(\\u0026#34;sqlServerDiagnosticSetting\\u0026#34;)\\n      {\\n          WorkspaceId = laWorkspace.Resource.WorkspaceId.AsProvisioningParameter(infra),\\n          Logs =\\n          {\\n              new LogSettings\\n              {\\n                  IsEnabled = true,\\n                  Category = \\u0026#34;SQLSecurityAuditEvents\\u0026#34;,\\n              },\\n          },\\n      };\\n\\n      // You\\u0026#39;d need to deploy the project to see the end result, but you\\u0026#39;ll need to uncomment the below bit to ensure the diagnosticSetting is scoped to the intended database\\n      // var scopeValue = new BicepValue(new IdentifierExpression(database.BicepIdentifier));\\n      // ((IBicepValue)scopeValue).Self = new BicepValueReference(diagnosticSetting, \\u0026#34;Scope\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;]);\\n      // diagnosticSetting.ProvisionableProperties[\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;] = scopeValue;\\n\\n      infra.Add(diagnosticSetting);\\n  });\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n.NET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.107\\n Commit:            b16286c228\\n Workload version:  10.0.100-manifests.ef86bccd\\n MSBuild version:   18.0.11+b16286c22\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26200\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-x64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.107\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed. 2026-04-24T21:57:13.0037562Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-24T21:54:57.648388114Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58668,\"title\":\"[BUG] DiagnosticSetting missing required Scope property\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Provisioning.Monitor\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhile a scope property isn\\u0026#39;t required for DiagnosticSetting resources, omitting it would just bind the diagnostic setting to the resource group during deployment which could be unintended by the user. In our case, we are trying to add it to a sql database which requires something like the below code to force in the scope property.\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar scopeValue = new BicepValue(new IdentifierExpression(database.BicepIdentifier));\\n((IBicepValue)scopeValue).Self = new BicepValueReference(diagnosticSetting, \\u0026#34;Scope\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;]);\\ndiagnosticSetting.ProvisionableProperties[\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;] = scopeValue;\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe DiagnosticSetting class should support a Scope field to allow binding the resource to the correct parent resource.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nDepending on whether you force in the scope field or not, you could end up with a diagnostic setting on the resource group unintentionally.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nCreate a simple Aspire application using a default template.\\nAdd a sql server resource and a database\\nInside the ConfigureInfrastructure callback (on the server), add the diagnostic setting, then just do `aspire publish` to get the generated bicep.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; You\\u0026#39;ll need to have a dev environment setup to run Aspire projects. If all you\\u0026#39;re doing is executing aspire publish the Aspire CLI would be sufficient. If you\\u0026#39;re going to run the project you\\u0026#39;ll need Docker, at least for how the below code is written.\\n\\nSample snippet - add this in your Aspire App Host\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar laWorkspace = builder.AddAzureLogAnalyticsWorkspace(\\u0026#34;log-analytics\\u0026#34;)\\nvar azureSql = builder.AddAzureSqlServer(\\u0026#34;azuresql\\u0026#34;).RunAsContainer(c =\\u0026gt; c.WithLifetime(ContainerLifetime.Persistent));\\nvar db = azureSql.AddDatabase(\\u0026#34;mydb\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;MyDB\\u0026#34;);\\nazureSql.ConfigureInfrastructure(infra =\\u0026gt;\\n  {\\n      var database = infra.GetProvisionableResources().OfType().Single();\\n      var diagnosticSetting = new DiagnosticSetting(\\u0026#34;sqlServerDiagnosticSetting\\u0026#34;)\\n      {\\n          WorkspaceId = laWorkspace.Resource.WorkspaceId.AsProvisioningParameter(infra),\\n          Logs =\\n          {\\n              new LogSettings\\n              {\\n                  IsEnabled = true,\\n                  Category = \\u0026#34;SQLSecurityAuditEvents\\u0026#34;,\\n              },\\n          },\\n      };\\n\\n      // You\\u0026#39;d need to deploy the project to see the end result, but you\\u0026#39;ll need to uncomment the below bit to ensure the diagnosticSetting is scoped to the intended database\\n      // var scopeValue = new BicepValue(new IdentifierExpression(database.BicepIdentifier));\\n      // ((IBicepValue)scopeValue).Self = new BicepValueReference(diagnosticSetting, \\u0026#34;Scope\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;]);\\n      // diagnosticSetting.ProvisionableProperties[\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;] = scopeValue;\\n\\n      infra.Add(diagnosticSetting);\\n  });\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n.NET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.107\\n Commit:            b16286c228\\n Workload version:  10.0.100-manifests.ef86bccd\\n MSBuild version:   18.0.11+b16286c22\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26200\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-x64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.107\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed. 2026-04-24T21:57:13.0174590Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-24T21:57:02.995752161Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** The `DiagnosticSetting` class in `Azure.Provisioning.Monitor` lacks a `Scope` property, making it difficult to correctly bind a diagnostic setting to a specific resource (e.g., an Azure SQL Database) without resorting to low-level, internal workarounds.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.Monitor` (version not explicitly stated; environment shows .NET 10.0.107)\n**Affected API:** `DiagnosticSetting` class — missing `Scope` property\n**Scenarios:**\n- User wants to attach a `DiagnosticSetting` to a SQL Database resource in an Aspire application\n- Without `Scope`, the diagnostic setting silently binds to the resource group instead of the intended resource\n- Workaround requires using internal/undocumented APIs (`BicepValue`, `IBicepValue`, `BicepValueReference`, `ProvisionableProperties`)\n\n**Root ask:** Add a first-class `Scope` property to `DiagnosticSetting` so users can declaratively bind the diagnostic setting to the correct Azure resource.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe `DiagnosticSetting` resource in Bicep supports a `scope` property for extension resources. When omitted, ARM defaults the scope to the containing resource group. For child/extension resources like diagnostic settings, the scope must be set explicitly.\n\nThe workaround in the issue directly manipulates `ProvisionableProperties[\"scope\"]` using internal types, which is fragile and not user-friendly.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Confirm whether `DiagnosticSetting` in `Azure.Provisioning.Monitor` was generated from TypeSpec/ARM definitions and whether the `scope` property is present in the underlying ARM schema.\n2. Check if other extension-type resources in the Provisioning library expose a `Scope` property as a pattern to follow (e.g., `RoleAssignment`).\n3. Add a `Scope` property (of type `BicepValue\u003cResourceIdentifier\u003e` or equivalent) to `DiagnosticSetting` and verify generated Bicep output includes the correct `scope` field.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🏷️ Label Confidence\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Category:** `Provisioning` — The affected package is `Azure.Provisioning.Monitor`, which falls squarely in the Azure.Provisioning.* family.\n- **Service:** `Monitor` — The issue is about `DiagnosticSetting` within Azure Monitor's provisioning library.\n- **Confidence:** High — Package name is explicitly stated, category and service are unambiguous.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e👥 Owner Routing\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Monitor` (line 1034) — matched on the \"Monitor\" service label; this entry covers `Azure.ResourceManager.Monitor` and general Monitor service routing.\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed\n- **ServiceOwners:** @thomasp98296\n- **Routing action:** Added \"Service Attention\" and \"needs-team-attention\" labels; issue left unassigned per ServiceOwners-only routing.\n- **Scan notes:** Starting from bottom of CODEOWNERS — `%CodeGen %Mgmt` (line ~1210), `%CodeGen` (line ~1206), and other non-matching entries were skipped before reaching `%Monitor` at line 1034 which matched on the \"Monitor\" label.
